notes: Updated Mar 15 ~10:30 AM ET. Sources: CNBC, CNN, Al Jazeera, ABC News, CBS News, Iran International, Wikipedia, Semafor.
Daily launch series: Feb 28–Mar 9 reconstruction; Mar 10–11 UAE official; Mar 12–13 fragment est. Mar 14–16 null. White House updated: ballistic ↓90%, drones ↓95%, 65+ vessels sunk, 6,000+ targets.
Oil: markets closed today (Sunday). Brent closed Fri $103.14. WTI $98.71. Fujairah oil-loading resumed after drone debris interruption.
Iran killed: 1,400+ (AJ tracker). 10,000+ residential homes damaged/destroyed (Tehran governor). 56 cultural sites damaged (Heritage Ministry). Lebanon: 773+.

MAJOR: 90+ military targets on Kharg Island struck Fri night (CENTCOM). Isfahan struck Sunday AM — 15+ killed. Iran fired 10+ hypersonic missiles at Israel. Israel critically low on interceptors (Semafor).
Business/shipping/publishing sections reflect on-ground intel + sourced analysis. Not legal advice.

metrics.label: Conflict day
metrics.desc: Since Feb 28. Third week. All chart data now continuous D1–D16.

metrics.label: Brent crude
metrics.desc: Fri close. Markets closed today (Sun). Fujairah loading resumed.

metrics.label: Kharg Island
metrics.desc: CENTCOM Fri night. Oil infra spared but explicitly threatened.

metrics.label: Isfahan
metrics.desc: Heavy strikes early Sun AM. 15+ killed. Dozens of impacts.

metrics.label: Israel interceptors
metrics.desc: Israel told US running low on ballistic missile interceptors (Semafor).

metrics.label: US military deaths
metrics.desc: 6 KC-135; 6 Kuwait; 1 Iraq. 3 more injured Kuwait Sat.

metrics.label: Hypersonic missiles
metrics.desc: IRGC fired 10+ hypersonic missiles at Israel today. Bnei Brak hit.

sitrep: Day 16 — Isfahan struck, hypersonic missiles fired: Early Sunday, heavy US-Israeli airstrikes hit multiple sites across Isfahan province, killing at least 15. Simultaneously: 20 explosions near Shiraz, heavy strikes in southern Tehran, Dezful Air Base, Khomein, Hamedan, and Jask port (Hormozgan). This is the broadest single-day geographic spread of strikes so far.

Kharg Island — 90+ targets confirmed: CENTCOM confirmed US forces struck more than 90 military targets on Kharg Island Friday night. Oil infrastructure deliberately preserved. White House restated: ballistic capability ↓90%, drones ↓95%, 65+ naval vessels sunk, 6,000+ targets struck total.

Iran fires hypersonic missiles: IRGC launched 10+ hypersonic missiles at Israel plus drones. Hit Bnei Brak apartment building — 4 injured. Also targeted Al-Dhafra Air Base in UAE. IRGC vowed to 'pursue and kill Netanyahu.' Sirens in central Israel.

Israel interceptor crisis: Israel informed the US it is running critically low on ballistic missile interceptors (Semafor). This is a strategic vulnerability — if interceptor stocks deplete, Iran's residual launch capability becomes far more dangerous.

Trump: not ready for deal: Told NBC Iran 'wants to make a deal' but terms 'aren't good enough yet.' Calling for naval coalition to keep Hormuz open. UK energy secretary Miliband: exploring 'any options' to help secure Hormuz.

Civilian toll deepening: Tehran governor: 10,000+ residential homes damaged or destroyed. 56 cultural sites damaged (Heritage Ministry). Brig. Gen. Jalali Nasab killed (another senior military figure). Pope Leo called for immediate ceasefire.
Reza Pahlavi: Exiled prince outlined 5 economic goals for post-regime transition — remove economy from IRGC control, address corruption. Signals US-backed regime change messaging accelerating.

Key signal: The combination of Kharg strike, Isfahan bombardment, AND Israel's interceptor crisis creates the most dangerous 48 hours of the conflict. Iran's hypersonic missiles are harder to intercept — if Israel's interceptor stocks are depleted, the calculus changes dramatically.

theoryEvaluation.title: Support
theoryEvaluation.body: White House confirms ballistic ↓90%, drones ↓95%. 90+ Kharg targets hit. 65+ vessels sunk. 6,000+ total targets. Industrial base being degraded (Tabriz factories, Jask port). Launch suppression is working at a mechanical level.

theoryEvaluation.title: Weakens confidence
theoryEvaluation.body: Iran fired 10+ HYPERSONIC missiles at Israel today. This is a capability escalation — hypersonics are harder to intercept. Combined with Israel's interceptor crisis (Semafor), the remaining Iranian capability is more dangerous per shot even if volume is down. Isfahan struck but Iran is hitting back. Trump 'not ready for deal' = no off-ramp being built.

theoryEvaluation.title: Current judgment
theoryEvaluation.body: The theory — 'can strikes suppress capability before costs force pullback?' — is now being tested at its sharpest edge. Capability IS down 90–95%. But the remaining 5–10% includes hypersonic missiles vs depleting Israeli interceptors, which is a worse ratio than 100% of capability vs full interceptor stocks. The war's most dangerous phase may be NOW, not Day 1.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Protracted low-intensity + periodic escalation spikes
scenarioProbabilities.body: The new baseline. Strikes continue but at variable intensity. Hypersonic missiles vs. depleting interceptors creates a dangerous equilibrium. Neither side can deliver knockout blow. Oil $95–115.

scenarioProbabilities.name: US 'Mission Accomplished' + wind-down
scenarioProbabilities.body: ↓ from 28%. Trump saying 'not ready for deal' pushes this back. But Kharg + interceptor crisis could force accelerated timeline if costs spike. Naval coalition talk = looking for exit that isn't 'retreat.'

scenarioProbabilities.name: Full economic/regional war escalation
scenarioProbabilities.body: Stable from yesterday. Kharg oil infrastructure is the red line. Araghchi warned: strike energy sites = Iran hits US companies' facilities. Israel interceptor depletion could trigger this scenario if a mass-casualty strike gets through.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Negotiated ceasefire
scenarioProbabilities.body: ↑ slightly. Pope Leo ceasefire call. Araghchi offering to 'cooperate with regional neighbors.' Iran's terms (guarantees + compensation) still on table. But Trump 'not ready' = no near-term deal.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Iranian regime fracture
scenarioProbabilities.body: Pahlavi's transition message = US amplifying regime change track. Khamenei still not appearing. But IRGC showing capability (hypersonic missiles) undermines collapse narrative.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Nuclear threshold
scenarioProbabilities.body: Unchanged. Parchin damaged. Hidden enriched uranium found pre-war.

dailySeries.sourceLabel: D1–D10: JPost verified (cumul 2,410 missiles / 3,560 drones). D11–12: UAE official. D13–14: fragment est. D15: UAE 9+33 (AJ) + Saudi 51 drones scaled. D16: hypersonic escalation est. All-theater estimates assume UAE receives ~48% of projectiles (INSS).

oil.note: Full daily series. CNBC confirmed where noted, interpolated between. Mar 15 = Fri close. Markets closed Sun Mar 16. Brent +10% weekly, second week >$100.

latestTargetBreakdown.note: Mar 14 slice. UAE: 9 missiles + 33 drones intercepted (AJ confirmed). Qatar: 4 ballistic + drones (MoD). Saudi: 51 drones intercepted (largest single-day barrage). Kuwait: airport drones. Jordan: 79/85 intercepted over week 2.

dailyRows.capability: Full inventory surge. Khamenei assassinated. IRGC closes Hormuz.
dailyRows.cost: Oil $72. True Promise IV within hours.
Dubai Airport struck. Oil $79. Khamenei death confirmed.
Hezbollah enters. 20 killed Tehran. PJAK enters Iran.

dailyRows.capability: Sliding. IRIB + parliament hit. IRIS Dena sunk.
dailyRows.cost: Trump: no navy left. Assembly of Experts bombed.

dailyRows.capability: CENTCOM: air dominance. ‘90% ballistic, ‘83% drones. Fars: 500+ missiles, ~2,000 drones cumul.
dailyRows.cost: Azadi Complex bombed. Azerbaijan airport hit.
Trump demands surrender. Oil $120 intraday. Iran mines Hormuz. Iraq ‘70%.
WTI $119→$85 crash. G7 release. Falak-ol-Aflak struck.
Isfahan heritage damaged. WTI biggest weekly gain ever.
IEA 400M barrel release. 16 minelayers sunk. Iran threatens banks.
Khamenei: Hormuz stays closed. 3.2M displaced. Brent $100.46. Dow ‘700.

dailyRows.capability: Degraded not eliminated. IRGC-Hezbollah joint ops.
dailyRows.cost: Tehran strikes during rallies. KC-135 crash. 13 US deaths. Lebanon 773 killed.

dailyRows.primary: UAE 9+33 (AJ). Saudi 51 drones (largest barrage). Qatar 4 ballistic. Kuwait airport drones.
dailyRows.capability: Kharg 90+ targets struck (CENTCOM). White House: ballistic ↓90%, drones ↓95%, 65+ vessels sunk. But drone volume ticked UP vs D13 — Saudi saw largest single-day barrage.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $103.14 close. Fujairah oil hub fire from drone debris. IRGC declares UAE bases 'legitimate targets.' $10M bounty on Khamenei. Trump 'not ready for deal.'
dailyRows.assessment: Kharg escalation + drone volume uptick to Saudi = war not winding down.

dailyRows.capability: IRGC fires 10+ HYPERSONIC missiles — capability escalation. Israel critically low on interceptors (Semafor). Heavy Isfahan strikes. Jask port, Dezful, Shiraz, Tehran, Hamedan all hit.
dailyRows.cost: 15+ killed Isfahan. 10,000+ homes destroyed (Tehran governor). 56 cultural sites damaged (Heritage Ministry). IRGC vows to kill Netanyahu. Pope calls ceasefire. Pahlavi outlines transition. UK exploring Hormuz options.
dailyRows.assessment: Hypersonic + interceptor crisis = war's most dangerous phase.

dailyRows.capability: IRGC fires 10+ HYPERSONIC missiles — capability escalation. Israel critically low on interceptors (Semafor). Heavy Isfahan strikes. Jask port, Dezful, Shiraz, Tehran, Hamedan all hit.
dailyRows.cost: 15+ killed Isfahan. 10,000+ homes destroyed (Tehran governor). 56 cultural sites damaged (Heritage Ministry). IRGC vows to kill Netanyahu. Pope calls ceasefire. Pahlavi outlines transition. UK exploring Hormuz options.
dailyRows.assessment: Hypersonic + interceptor crisis = war's most dangerous phase.

timeline.title: Immediate (next 48h)
timeline.body: CRITICAL: Israel's interceptor depletion vs Iran's hypersonic capability. If a mass-casualty missile gets through Israeli defenses, escalation ladder accelerates dramatically. Watch for emergency US interceptor resupply to Israel.

timeline.title: This week
timeline.body: IMO emergency session Mar 18–19 on Hormuz. Fed rate decision amid $103 oil. Naval coalition forming (Trump + UK exploring options). Markets reopen Monday into Kharg + Isfahan news.

timeline.title: Decision horizon (1–3 weeks)
timeline.body: Trump 'not ready for deal' pushes ceasefire window back. But interceptor crisis could force recalculation. Pahlavi's transition messaging = long-game regime change track being laid.

timeline.title: Medium-term
timeline.body: EIA: Brent >$95 through May. Hypersonic missiles change the interceptor math permanently — even post-war, Israel's defense architecture needs rethinking.

timeline.title: Main uncertainty
timeline.body: Israel's interceptor stock is the single most important variable. If it depletes, Iran's remaining 5–10% capability becomes existential rather than residual. This could trigger either emergency ceasefire pressure OR Israeli nuclear doctrine debates.

endstate.title: Most likely (shifting)
endstate.body: Protracted conflict with periodic escalation spikes rather than clean de-escalation. Neither side can deliver knockout blow. Iran's hypersonic capability + depleting Israeli interceptors = dangerous equilibrium rather than one-sided suppression.

endstate.title: Rising risk
endstate.body: A hypersonic missile penetrating Israeli defenses and causing mass casualties would be a transformative event. Could trigger Israeli nuclear doctrine debates, emergency US intervention, or forced ceasefire — all with massive second-order effects.

endstate.title: What would change this
endstate.body: Emergency US interceptor resupply restoring Israeli confidence; Iran running out of hypersonic stock (unknown quantity); a diplomatic breakthrough via regional mediator; or a catastrophic event (Kharg oil hit, nuclear escalation) that forces both sides to the table.

impactIranfarhang: SUPPLY CHAIN FREEZE — see Shipping Routes section below for route-by-route analysis. Original route (Tehran→Dubai→Atlanta via Emirates) is dead. Turkey land route is closed per on-ground reports despite official claims. Armenia (Agarak/Norduz) is the most viable alternative — border confirmed open — but the Iran-side domestic leg is the constraint.

PUBLISHING INDUSTRY IN CRISIS — see Publishing Status section below. Paper imports halted (Hormuz closed, rial collapsed). Internet blackout 2+ months (online sales ↓80%). Power outages disrupting printing. Distribution networks broken. New title production likely halted or severely reduced at most publishers. Only pre-war warehouse stock may be available.
OFAC exemption: STABLE (LOW risk). 31 CFR §560.210(c) is statutory — Berman Amendment / IEEPA. Not affected by war escalation.
Payment via KEMCO/France: MEDIUM-HIGH. EU banks watching Kharg escalation. If conflict hits oil infrastructure, sanctions environment could tighten.
Demand: POSITIVE. Academic interest in Iranian affairs at peak. Ship whatever Atlanta inventory you have.

Bottom line: The shipping problem is real but solvable (Armenia route). The publishing industry problem is deeper — even if you get a route, there may be limited new supply to move. Focus on: (1) clearing Atlanta inventory, (2) testing Armenia route for existing warehouse stock, (3) monitoring for any signs of publishing resumption.
prepIranfarhang: Ship existing Atlanta inventory now — demand is peaking.
Proactive client comms: wartime logistics, not compliance. Exemption intact.
Monitor: any Iran air-freight resumption is weeks away at minimum given Kharg escalation.
signalsIranfarhang: Iranian air-freight resumption (further away after Kharg).
EU banking actions on France-Iran corridors.
Any ceasefire/de-escalation signal (would be first supply chain indicator).
impactKIP: CRITICAL ESCALATION — IRGC declared UAE bases ‘legitimate targets’ after Kharg strike. This directly threatens the UAE commercial ecosystem. If IRGC follows through, Dubai-based operations face physical security risk beyond just financial/logistics disruption.
Iran-UAE corridor: NEAR-TOTAL DISRUPTION (unchanged). Hormuz closed. Jebel Ali reducing. 70%+ flights canceled.
UAE banking: HIGH and RISING. Kharg escalation increases probability of UAE cracking down on Iran-linked networks — both as retaliation and as defensive posture against IRGC threats.
Asset seizure: MEDIUM-HIGH (upgraded from MEDIUM). IRGC threatening UAE directly gives Emirati authorities stronger political cover and motivation for Iran-linked asset freezes.
Shipping: CRITICAL. Kharg threat to oil infra + IRGC $200 oil warning = worst-case shipping scenario becoming more plausible.
NEW: Physical security. IRGC designating UAE bases as targets means commercial zones near military facilities face collateral risk. Jebel Ali port is near Al Minhad Air Base.
prepKIP: URGENT: Reassess ALL UAE exposure — financial AND physical. IRGC’s ‘legitimate targets’ declaration changes risk profile.
Move liquid assets from UAE accounts NOW if not already done.
Document all records. UAE crackdown probability increased by Kharg escalation.
Turkey corridor is now the primary alternative. Begin operational shift.
Map physical proximity of any warehoused goods to military facilities.
Model complete UAE shutdown scenario — this is now the planning base case, not a tail risk.
Monitor IRGC follow-through on UAE target threats — any strike on UAE commercial infrastructure would trigger cascading shutdowns.
Monitor yuan-Hormuz — still possible but less likely near-term given Kharg escalation.
signalsKIP: IRGC strikes on UAE commercial or port infrastructure — would trigger immediate cascade.
UAE bank closures or transaction rejections for Iran-linked clients.
Jebel Ali cargo holds or port closure.
Hormuz mine incidents affecting commercial shipping.
Any Iran retaliation against Gulf oil facilities (Saudi Ras Tanura, UAE ADNOC, etc.).
Ceasefire signals (would be first positive indicator for corridor restoration).
theoryBox: Core question: can U.S./Israeli strikes suppress meaningful missile and drone launch capability before political and economic costs force them to back off? If yes, the regime becomes much more vulnerable.
theoryBox_fa: پرسش اصلی: آیا حملات آمریکا و اسرائیل می‌تواند توان پرتاب موشک و پهپاد ایران را پیش از آنکه هزینه‌های سیاسی و اقتصادی آنها را عقب براند، سرکوب کند؟

metrics_fa.label: نفت برنت
metrics_fa.desc: بسته شدن جمعه. بازار امروز (یکشنبه) تعطیل.

metrics_fa.label: جزیره خارگ
metrics_fa.desc: جمعه شب. تأسیسات نفتی حفظ اما تهدید شد.

metrics_fa.label: رهگیرهای اسرائیل
metrics_fa.desc: اسرائیل به آمریکا اطلاع داد رهگیرهای موشک بالستیک رو به اتمام است.

metrics_fa.label: موشک مافوق‌صوت
metrics_fa.desc: سپاه ۱۰+ موشک مافوق‌صوت به اسرائیل شلیک کرد.

sitrep_fa: روز ۱۶ — حمله به اصفهان، موشک‌های مافوق‌صوت: صبح یکشنبه، حملات سنگین به اصفهان — حداقل ۱۵ کشته. همزمان ۲۰ انفجار نزدیک شیراز، جنوب تهران، پایگاه دزفول، خمین، همدان و بندر جاسک. گسترده‌ترین پراکندگی جغرافیایی حملات.
خارگ — ۹۰+ هدف تأیید شد: CENTCOM: بیش از ۹۰ هدف نظامی در خارگ جمعه شب. تأسیسات نفتی حفظ شد. کاخ سفید: بالستیک ↓۹۰٪، پهپاد ↓۹۵٪، ۶۵+ ناو غرق‌شده.
ایران موشک مافوق‌صوت شلیک کرد: سپاه ۱۰+ موشک مافوق‌صوت به اسرائیل. آپارتمان بنی‌براک آسیب دید — ۴ زخمی. تهدید به کشتن نتانیاهو.
بحران رهگیر اسرائیل: اسرائیل به آمریکا گفت موجودی رهگیرهای بالستیک بحرانی است. اگر تمام شود، توان باقیمانده ایران بسیار خطرناک‌تر می‌شود.
ترامپ: آماده معامله نیست: ایران می‌خواهد معامله کند اما شرایط قابل قبول نیست. خواستار ائتلاف دریایی برای بازگشایی هرمز.
تلفات غیرنظامی: ۱۰,۰۰۰+ خانه تخریب شده (استاندار تهران). ۵۶ سایت فرهنگی آسیب‌دیده. پاپ لئو خواستار آتش‌بس فوری شد.
رضا پهلوی: ۵ هدف اقتصادی برای دوران گذار. نشان‌دهنده تسریع پیام تغییر رژیم.
نشانه کلیدی: ترکیب حمله خارگ + بمباران اصفهان + بحران رهگیر اسرائیل خطرناک‌ترین ۴۸ ساعت جنگ را ایجاد کرده.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: جنگ کم‌شدت طولانی + نوسانات تشدید
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: خط پایه جدید. حملات ادامه دارد اما با شدت متغیر. موشک مافوق‌صوت در برابر رهگیرهای رو به اتمام.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: اعلام «مأموریت انجام شد»
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: ترامپ گفت آماده معامله نیست. اما بحران رهگیر ممکن است جدول زمانی را تسریع کند.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: جنگ اقتصادی/منطقه‌ای تمام‌عیار
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: عراقچی هشدار داد: حمله به تأسیسات انرژی = حمله به تأسیسات شرکت‌های آمریکایی.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: آتش‌بس مذاکره‌ای
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: پاپ لئو خواستار آتش‌بس شد. عراقچی پیشنهاد همکاری با همسایگان. اما ترامپ آماده نیست.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: شکست داخلی رژیم
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: پیام پهلوی = تقویت مسیر تغییر رژیم. اما توان مافوق‌صوت سپاه روایت فروپاشی را تضعیف می‌کند.

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: حمایت
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: کاخ سفید: بالستیک ↓۹۰٪، پهپاد ↓۹۵٪. ۹۰+ هدف خارگ. ۶۵+ ناو غرق‌شده. سرکوب پرتاب در سطح مکانیکی کار می‌کند.

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: تضعیف اطمینان
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: ایران ۱۰+ موشک مافوق‌صوت شلیک کرد — تشدید توانمندی. با بحران رهگیر اسرائیل، توان باقیمانده ایران خطرناک‌تر است. ترامپ آماده معامله نیست.

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: ارزیابی
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: فرضیه در حادترین نقطه آزمایش می‌شود. توان ↓۹۰-۹۵٪ اما ۵-۱۰٪ باقیمانده شامل مافوق‌صوت در برابر رهگیرهای رو به اتمام است. خطرناک‌ترین فاز جنگ ممکن است الان باشد.

timeline_fa.title: فوری (۴۸ ساعت)
timeline_fa.body: بحرانی: رهگیرهای اسرائیل در برابر مافوق‌صوت ایران. اگر یک موشک از سپر دفاعی عبور کند، تشدید شتاب می‌گیرد.

timeline_fa.title: این هفته
timeline_fa.body: IMO ۱۸–۱۹ مارس. فدرال رزرو. ائتلاف دریایی. بازار دوشنبه باز می‌شود.

timeline_fa.title: افق تصمیم
timeline_fa.body: ترامپ آماده معامله نیست. اما بحران رهگیر ممکن است بازنگری ایجاد کند.

timeline_fa.title: میان‌مدت
timeline_fa.body: موشک مافوق‌صوت معادله رهگیری را عوض کرده — حتی بعد از جنگ.

timeline_fa.title: عدم‌قطعیت
timeline_fa.body: موجودی رهگیر اسرائیل مهم‌ترین متغیر است.

endstate_fa.title: محتمل‌ترین
endstate_fa.body: جنگ طولانی با نوسانات تشدید. هیچ طرف ضربه قاطع نمی‌زند. تعادل خطرناک مافوق‌صوت/رهگیر.

endstate_fa.title: ریسک رو به افزایش
endstate_fa.body: عبور موشک مافوق‌صوت از سپر اسرائیل رویداد تحول‌آفرین خواهد بود.

endstate_fa.title: چه چیزی تغییر می‌دهد
endstate_fa.body: تأمین مجدد رهگیر آمریکایی، اتمام موجودی مافوق‌صوت ایران، یا رویداد فاجعه‌بار.

impactIranfarhang_fa: زنجیره تأمین یخ‌زده — بخش مسیرهای حمل را ببینید. مسیر اصلی (تهران←دبی←آتلانتا) بسته. ترکیه زمینی بسته (گزارش میدانی). ارمنستان (آگاراک/نوردوز) بهترین جایگزین — مرز باز — اما پای ایرانی گلوگاه است.
صنعت نشر در بحران — بخش وضعیت نشر را ببینید. واردات کاغذ متوقف. اینترنت ۲+ ماه قطع. قطع برق چاپ را مختل کرده. تولید عنوان جدید احتمالاً متوقف یا به‌شدت کند شده.
معافیت OFAC: پایدار. قانونی است و تحت تأثیر تشدید جنگ نیست.
پرداخت KEMCO/فرانسه: متوسط-بالا. بانک‌های اروپایی وضعیت خارگ را رصد می‌کنند.
خلاصه: مشکل حمل حل‌شدنی است (مسیر ارمنستان). مشکل نشر عمیق‌تر است — حتی با مسیر باز، عرضه جدید محدود است. تمرکز: ارسال موجودی آتلانتا، تست مسیر ارمنستان، رصد نشانه‌های بازگشت نشر.
prepIranfarhang_fa: موجودی آتلانتا را فوراً ارسال کنید.
اطلاع‌رسانی فعال به مشتریان: اختلال لجستیکی جنگ، نه مشکل تطابق.
منابع ناشران مهاجر (ترکیه) را بررسی کنید.
سوابق پشتیبان خارج از ایران نگه دارید.
ساختار پرداخت KEMCO/فرانسه را تغییر ندهید.
signalsIranfarhang_fa: از سرگیری حمل هوایی ایران (حتی جزئی).
impactKIP_fa: تشدید بحرانی — سپاه پایگاه‌های امارات را «اهداف مشروع» اعلام کرد. این مستقیماً اکوسیستم تجاری امارات را تهدید می‌کند.
کریدور ایران-امارات: اختلال تقریباً کامل. هرمز بسته. جبل‌علی کاهش یافته. ۷۰٪+ پروازها لغو.
بانکداری امارات: بالا و رو به افزایش. حمله خارگ احتمال سرکوب شبکه‌های مرتبط با ایران را افزایش داد.
توقیف دارایی: متوسط-بالا (از کم ارتقا یافته). سپاه مستقیماً امارات را تهدید کرده.
حمل‌ونقل: بحرانی. اطلاعیه‌های پایان سفر. تخلیه در بنادر جایگزین.
prepKIP_fa: فوری: تمام دارایی‌های امارات را ارزیابی کنید — مالی و فیزیکی.
دارایی‌های نقدی را از حساب‌های امارات خارج کنید.
سناریوی تعطیلی کامل امارات را برنامه‌ریزی کنید.
اعلامیه‌های نظارتی امارات را روزانه رصد کنید.
signalsKIP_fa: حمله سپاه به زیرساخت تجاری امارات.
بستن حساب بانکی یا رد تراکنش.
اعلام رسمی امارات درباره توقیف دارایی‌های مرتبط با ایران.
توقف یا تغییر مسیر محموله‌ها در جبل‌علی.
هرگونه سیگنال آتش‌بس یا بازگشایی هرمز.

shippingRoutes.detail: Iranian airspace = active combat zone (~1,000 daily strikes). No commercial flights in/out of Iran. Dubai Airport hit by drones multiple times. Emirates grounded most routes. Even post-war, Iran-UAE political relations fundamentally damaged — Iran struck UAE soil (6 killed, 131 wounded), UAE withdrew ambassador, IRGC declared UAE bases 'legitimate targets' after Kharg strike. Emirates restoring an Iran cargo route would be a political decision, not just operational. Treat as months away at best, possibly permanently altered.
shippingRoutes.eta: Months at minimum. Possibly never returns to pre-war form.

shippingRoutes.route: Iran → Turkey (land cargo) → Istanbul air freight → Atlanta
shippingRoutes.detail: CONFIRMED CLOSED per on-the-ground sources as of mid-March, despite Turkish Trade Minister's Mar 2 claim that cargo 'continues in a controlled manner.' The disconnect between official statements and reality is significant. Likely causes: Iran-side logistics collapse (internet blackout, domestic freight disrupted), Iranian exit restrictions, and de facto border closure for commercial goods even if checkpoints are technically open for passenger transit. Additionally, Tabriz — on the Tehran-Turkey freight corridor — was bombed Mar 15 (industrial targets). Isfahan (also on east-west transit routes) hit heavily today. Iran's internal freight network is increasingly degraded by strikes on industrial and transport infrastructure.
shippingRoutes.eta: Requires: (1) actual Iran-side freight operations resuming, (2) Tehran-to-border domestic logistics functioning, (3) Tabriz corridor safe for cargo. Weeks to months.

shippingRoutes.route: Iran → Armenia (Agarak/Norduz land border) → Yerevan air freight → Atlanta
shippingRoutes.detail: US Embassy (Virtual Embassy Iran) confirms Agarak/Norduz border open in EVERY daily security alert (Mar 8–14, verified). Armenian Mirror-Spectator reporting from the border on Mar 12: checkpoint 'open and operating normally.' Normal flow: 400–450 trucks/day. Briefly closed Feb 28 (Day 1), reopened quickly. Armenia is NOT a party to the conflict and has not been targeted. Yerevan has air freight connections — likely via European hub (Frankfurt, Vienna) to Atlanta. The bottleneck is the SAME as Turkey: getting books from Tehran to the Iran-Armenia border (~700km through northwestern Iran, partially in strike zones). But the border itself and the Armenia-side onward chain are the most stable options available.
shippingRoutes.viability: ~40% (border open; Iran-side domestic leg is the constraint)
shippingRoutes.eta: Could work NOW if Iran-side logistics partner can move goods from Tehran to Norduz. Requires landline/in-person coordination due to internet blackout.

shippingRoutes.detail: US Embassy explicitly warns against Iran-Pakistan border area. Turkmenistan requires special government authorization. Afghanistan is a non-starter. These are not viable commercial freight routes for books.

shippingRoutes_fa.detail: حریم هوایی ایران منطقه جنگی فعال. هیچ پروازی تجاری ندارد. فرودگاه دبی بارها مورد حمله پهپادی قرار گرفته. حتی بعد از جنگ، روابط سیاسی ایران-امارات به‌شدت آسیب دیده. ماه‌ها فاصله دارد.
shippingRoutes_fa.eta: حداقل ماه‌ها. ممکن است هرگز به شکل قبل برنگردد.

shippingRoutes_fa.detail: وزیر تجارت ترکیه ادعا کرد حمل‌ونقل تجاری ادامه دارد، اما گزارش‌های میدانی نشان می‌دهد مسیر عملاً بسته است. تبریز — در مسیر ترانزیت — بمباران شده (کارخانه‌های صنعتی).

shippingRoutes_fa.status: بهترین گزینه — مرز باز تأیید شده
shippingRoutes_fa.detail: سفارت آمریکا تأیید کرده مرز آگاراک/نوردوز در تمام به‌روزرسانی‌های روزانه (۸–۱۴ مارس) باز است. ارمنستان طرف جنگ نیست. گلوگاه: انتقال کتاب از تهران به مرز ایران-ارمنستان (~۷۰۰ کیلومتر) در شرایط جنگی و قطع اینترنت.
shippingRoutes_fa.eta: اگر شریک لجستیکی در ایران بتواند کالا را به نوردوز برساند، الان ممکن است.

shippingRoutes_fa.detail: مسیرهای تجاری قابل اتکا برای کتاب نیستند.

publishingStatus: Paper supply: CRITICAL. Iran does not produce its own paper pulp — it relies entirely on imports paid in foreign currency. The rial was already in freefall pre-war (protests crashed it further in Jan 2026). Now with Hormuz closed and foreign trade frozen, paper imports are effectively halted. Even before the war, publishers like Ofoq and Houpa warned they were running on stored paper stocks that would run out 'in a few months.' Those months may now be up.

Internet blackout: DEVASTATING for publishing operations. Iran's internet has been down since January 8 (originally for protests, extended through the war — now 2+ months). Per Wikipedia's article on the blackout: online sales dropped 80%, payment systems disrupted, $35–37M/day economic cost. Publishers cannot process orders, coordinate with authors, manage distribution, or conduct any digital business. The national intranet (NIN) was also fully disconnected at points.

Power outages: DISRUPTING printing. Widespread power outages reported in Tehran following air strikes (multiple sources). Printing presses require stable electricity. Even publishers with paper in stock may be unable to print. No data on which specific printing facilities have been damaged or are operational.

Distribution networks: BROKEN AND WORSENING. 3.2M displaced. Isfahan — Iran's second cultural capital and a major publishing center — was heavily bombed today (Mar 15). Tabriz hit yesterday. Jask port struck. Iran's Cultural Heritage Ministry confirmed 56 cultural sites damaged across Kurdistan, Lorestan, Kermanshah, Tehran, and Isfahan. Book markets, libraries, and publishing warehouses in these cities are at risk.

Book fairs: DISRUPTED. Publishing Perspectives (Mar 4) reported spring book fair season severely impacted. Muscat fair (Mar 26–Apr 5), Erbil, Abu Dhabi (April), and Doha (May) fairs all in question. Lebanese publishers' books in transit to Muscat may be stranded. London Book Fair travel snarled for anyone routing through Gulf hubs.

Pre-war baseline was already fragile. Iranian publishing was struggling before the war: paper costs soaring (200-page novel went from $1.60 to $4.10), small publishers being eliminated, readers priced out. The war has layered physical destruction, power outages, communication blackout, and currency collapse on top of an already weakened industry.

What this means for Iranfarhang sourcing: Even if a shipping route opens (Armenia being the best candidate), the question becomes: are publishers in Tehran actually producing new books? The combination of paper shortage, power outages, internet blackout, and workforce displacement suggests new title production has slowed dramatically or halted at many publishers. Existing warehouse stock (books already printed pre-war) is the only near-term supply — and that stock is finite and may be damaged or inaccessible.
publishingStatus_fa: تأمین کاغذ: بحرانی. ایران خمیر کاغذ تولید نمی‌کند — وابسته به واردات با ارز خارجی است. ریال قبل از جنگ سقوط کرده بود. با بسته شدن هرمز، واردات کاغذ عملاً متوقف شده.
قطع اینترنت: ویرانگر. اینترنت ایران از ۸ ژانویه قطع است (۲+ ماه). فروش آنلاین ۸۰٪ کاهش یافته. ناشران نمی‌توانند سفارش پردازش کنند یا با نویسندگان هماهنگ شوند.
قطع برق: اختلال در چاپ. قطعی گسترده برق در تهران پس از حملات هوایی. چاپخانه‌ها به برق پایدار نیاز دارند.
شبکه‌های توزیع: مختل. ۳.۲ میلیون آواره. حمل‌ونقل داخلی مختل. تبریز بمباران شده. وضعیت خیابان انقلاب و شهر کتاب: نامشخص.
نمایشگاه‌های کتاب: مختل. نمایشگاه‌های مسقط، اربیل، ابوظبی و دوحه همه زیر سؤال هستند.
صنعت نشر قبل از جنگ هم شکننده بود. هزینه کاغذ سر به فلک کشیده بود. ناشران کوچک از بازار حذف شده بودند. جنگ تخریب فیزیکی، قطع برق، قطع اینترنت و سقوط ارز را بر صنعت ضعیف‌شده تحمیل کرده.

معنای این برای ایران‌فرهنگ: حتی اگر مسیر حمل باز شود، سؤال اساسی این است: آیا ناشران در تهران واقعاً کتاب جدید تولید می‌کنند؟ ترکیب کمبود کاغذ، قطع برق، قطع اینترنت و آوارگی نیروی کار نشان می‌دهد تولید عنوان جدید به‌شدت کند شده یا متوقف شده. موجودی انبار (کتاب‌های چاپ‌شده قبل از جنگ) تنها منبع کوتاه‌مدت است.
